2026-03-26T03:37:46.3745866Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3746290Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3746660Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3746970Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3747208Z [36;1m[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3747382Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3747572Z [36;1m[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3747756Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3747993Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3748197Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3748500Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-03-26T03:37:46.3748779Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-03-26T03:41:04.6858416Z | # | Severity | Issue |

2026-03-26T03:41:08.3379956Z 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3380298Z ✓ $ cat /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js/sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth/package.json
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3380655Z 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3380910Z Agent: Now I have a good picture of the PR. Let me analyze the public API for potential issues:
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3381311Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3382745Z Agent: 1. **`configureEntraAuthentication` return type**: The function returns `typeof sequelizeInstance`. The issue here is that `typeof sequelizeInstance` is the type of the variable, not a stable type alias. This is unusual - typically we'd return the same type as the input parameter type (`SequelizeBeforeConnectHook`). However, this is intentional for chaining - it returns the original concrete Sequelize type, not just the interface.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3384195Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3385004Z Agent: 2. **`GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions` naming**: In `index.ts`, `GetEntraAccessTokenOptions` is exported as `GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions`:
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3385587Z Agent:    
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3386708Z Agent:    The internal type name doesn't match the exported name - it's called `GetEntraAccessTokenOptions` but exposed as `GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions`. Since the function is `entraTokenProvider` but the options type has "Password" in its name, there's a confusing mismatch between what the code actually does and what it's called.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3387822Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3388699Z Agent: 3. **Function naming `entraTokenProvider`**: The function is named `entraTokenProvider` but it returns a `Promise<string>` (the token). The naming suggests it's a higher-order function that returns a provider, when really it's just a provider function itself.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3389622Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3390580Z Agent: Looking at how it's actually used - `password: () => entraTokenProvider(credential)` - it's clear the function returns the token directly, not a provider of a provider. Something like `getEntraToken` or `getEntraTokenPassword` would be much clearer about what it does.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3391605Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3392214Z Agent: 4. **`SequelizeBeforeConnectHook` naming**: The name suggests this is a Sequelize-specific hook, but it's a structural type. It could be more generic.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3392912Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3393726Z Agent: 5. **Error handling uses raw `Error`**: The `configureEntraAuthentication` function throws `new Error("credential is required")` instead of using the Azure SDK error types. But this might be acceptable for a utility package.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3394814Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3395392Z Agent: 6. **`configureEntraAuthentication` doesn't extend `OperationOptions`**: The `ConfigureEntraAuth... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 7291 chars]
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3395936Z 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3396501Z ✓ $ cat /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js/.github/prompts/architecture-review-guidelines.md 2>/dev/null | head -200
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3397017Z 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3398099Z ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js && git --no-pager diff origin/main..HEAD -- sdk/postgresql/postgresql-auth/review/postgresql-auth-node.api.md 2>/dev/null | head -80
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3398967Z 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3399159Z Agent: Now I have a clear picture. Let me summarize the findings:
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3399503Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3400992Z Agent: 1. **`entraTokenProvider` naming** - the function acquires and returns a token (string), not a provider. Per Azure SDK guidelines, banned method prefixes include: `make`, `fetch`, `push`, `pop`, `getAll`, `erase`, `updateOrInsert`, `insertOrUpdate`. The preferred verbs are: `create`, `upsert`, `get`, `list`, `update`, `delete`, `send`, `set`, `remove`, `begin`. So `entraTokenProvider` should be `getEntraToken` or similar. 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3402404Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3404022Z Agent: 2. **`GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions` doesn't extend `OperationOptions`** - the `entraTokenProvider` function communicates with Azure AD (via `credential.getToken`). Its options bag should extend `OperationOptions` to support abort signals, tracing, and other standard Azure SDK operation options. Per the guidelines: "Every async public method must accept cancellation via `AbortSignal` (typically through an options bag that extends `OperationOptions`)."
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3406049Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3406730Z Agent: 3. **`ConfigureEntraAuthenticationOptions` doesn't extend `OperationOptions`** - this one also needs the same treatment since it's part of the async operation chain.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3407449Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3408323Z Agent: 4. **Return type `typeof sequelizeInstance`** - using `typeof` on a variable in the return type is unconventional. A generic approach would be clearer and more idiomatic, letting TypeScript infer the exact type of the instance being passed in.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3409293Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3410234Z Agent: 5. **Export alias naming inconsistency** - `GetEntraAccessTokenOptions` is exported as `GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions`, but the "Password" suffix conflicts with the function name `entraTokenProvider`. The naming should align across the API surface.
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3411232Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:41:08.3412725Z Agent: Looking at the most critical issues: the function naming doesn't follow verb conventions, the options interface is missing `OperationOptions` extension for AbortSignal support, and the return type could be more idiomatic. I'll focus my review comments on the API report file at the function signatures and interface definitions, plus the source files where the actual implementations live. Conti... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2730 chars]

When the workflow is configured with `target: \"*\"`, you must specify `pull_request_number` to indicate which PR to target. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 10 review comment(s) can be created. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["path","line","body"],"properties":{"path":{"type":"string","description":"File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js'). Must be a file that was changed in the PR."},"line":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"Line number for the comment. For single-line comments, this is the target line. For multi-line comments, this is the ending line."},"body":{"type":"string","description":"Review comment content in Markdown. Provide specific, actionable feedback about the code at this location."},"pull_request_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"Pull request number to add the review comment to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 876 in github.com/owner/repo/pull/876). If omitted, adds the comment to the PR that triggered this workflow. Required when the workflow target is '*' (any PR) — omitting it will cause the comment to fail."},"start_line":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"Starting line number for multi-line comments. When set, the comment spans from start_line to line. If you don't call this tool, review comments are still submitted as a COMMENT review. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.\n 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.\n 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. 2026-03-26T03:41:08.5952711Z Found gateway.md (11750 bytes)
2026-03-26T03:41:08.6020802Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-03-26T03:41:08.6021057Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-03-26T03:41:08.6021199Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-03-26T03:41:08.6021421Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-03-26T03:41:08.6021740Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-03-26T03:41:08.6021859Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
2026-03-26T03:41:08.6021993Z [36;1m  awf logs summary | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"[0m
2026-03-26T03:41:08.6022079Z [36;1melse[0m
